tasks.description: Review and approve the comprehensive technical requirements for the MRE signal flow visualization system that handles 676 tickers across 5 strategies. This approval gates the entire architecture — database design, WebSocket infrastructure, and UI framework decisions all depend on these core requirements being validated.
acceptanceCriteria: Review graph database + time-series architecture proposal (Neo4j + InfluxDB)
Approve multi-tiered WebSocket refresh rates (100ms ticks, 1s signals, 5s attribution)
Validate D3-React hybrid approach for 676+ node performance
Sign off on progressive disclosure UX for modal attribution details

tasks.text: Design comprehensive database schema for MRE signal flow with graph and time-series components
tasks.resolution: Created comprehensive database schema specification at ~/clawd/clawdbot-hub/public/data/specs/mre-signal-flow-db-schema.md. Includes Neo4j graph schema, InfluxDB time-series schema, sample queries, and cross-database join patterns.
tasks.description: Design a comprehensive database schema that combines graph relationships (Neo4j) with time-series performance data (InfluxDB) for the MRE signal flow system. The schema must handle real-time data from 676 tickers across 5 strategies while supporting complex queries for attribution analysis and correlation mapping.
acceptanceCriteria: Neo4j graph schema designed for strategy-ticker relationships and signal flow paths
InfluxDB time-series schema designed for high-frequency price/signal data with multiple granularities
Sample queries documented for common attribution scenarios (win rate, correlation, drawdown)
Cross-database query patterns defined for graph+time-series joins

tasks.text: Build multi-tiered WebSocket infrastructure for real-time signal updates
tasks.resolution: Created WebSocket architecture specification at ~/clawd/clawdbot-hub/public/data/specs/mre-signal-flow-websocket-arch.md. Includes multi-tiered refresh rates, Redis pub/sub design, message buffering, rate limiting, and fallback systems.
tasks.description: Build a multi-tiered WebSocket infrastructure with Redis pub/sub buffering to handle real-time updates for 676 tickers without overwhelming clients. The system needs different refresh rates: 100ms for price ticks, 1s for signals, 5s for attribution data, with intelligent message queuing and fallback mechanisms.
acceptanceCriteria: Redis pub/sub messaging system implemented with topic-based routing
Tiered refresh rate system: 100ms ticks, 1s signals, 5s attribution with client subscription management
Message buffering and burst handling to prevent frame drops during high-volume periods
Fallback polling system activated if WebSocket connections fail

tasks.text: Create D3-React hybrid visualization with modal detail system
tasks.resolution: Created D3-React UI specification at ~/clawd/clawdbot-hub/public/data/specs/mre-signal-flow-ui-spec.md. Includes component hierarchy, layout algorithms, modal system design, progressive disclosure system, and performance optimization strategies.
tasks.description: Create a D3-React hybrid visualization system that renders interactive flowcharts for 676+ tickers with modal detail views. D3 handles the graph performance while React manages modal state and UI interactions. The system must support force-directed layouts, filtering, and progressive disclosure without browser performance degradation.
acceptanceCriteria: D3.js force-directed graph layout implemented for 676+ nodes with optimized rendering
React modal system designed with strategy-ticker detail views and attribution metrics
Component hierarchy established with clean D3-React boundaries (D3 owns SVG, React owns UI)
Interactive filtering system: by strategy, by performance, by sector with persistent session state

tasks.text: Define attribution metrics specification for strategy-ticker modals
tasks.resolution: Created attribution metrics specification at ~/clawd/clawdbot-hub/public/data/specs/mre-signal-flow-attribution-metrics.md. Includes win rate analysis, return metrics, drawdown calculations, correlation analysis, regime dependency, and modal display specifications.
tasks.description: Define a comprehensive attribution metrics specification for strategy-ticker modal views that connects signal flow to P&L impact. Metrics include win rate, average return per signal, maximum drawdown periods, and portfolio correlation analysis with configurable timeframes (daily, weekly, monthly views).
acceptanceCriteria: Win rate calculation defined: successful signals vs. total signals per strategy-ticker pair
Return metrics specified: average return, max gain, max loss per signal with time decay analysis
Drawdown analysis: maximum drawdown period, recovery time, and frequency per strategy
Correlation metrics: strategy-to-portfolio correlation, ticker cross-correlations, and regime dependency

Strategy Versioning Data Model — Create strategy-versions.json schema with semver per strategy, release dates, changelog entries, and accuracy metrics. Include migration from current hardcoded strategy configs.
Strategy Release History UI — Add version badge + expandable changelog to each strategy modal header. Show current version, last updated date, and accuracy delta from previous version.

Per-Strategy Accuracy Tracking Pipeline — Compute and store accuracy % per strategy version from backtest results. Track win rate, Sharpe, and profit factor per version. Detect regressions automatically.
Backtest Integration for Strategy Modals — Surface per-strategy backtest results in the UI. Show historical performance charts, threshold optimization results, and per-asset-class breakdown.
